Rush (2013)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $26,947,624Details
International Box Office $71,211,580
Worldwide Box Office $98,159,204
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $5,089,976
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $7,659,290
Total $12,749,266
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Set against the sexy and glamorous "golden age" of Formula 1 racing, Rush portrays the true story of the rivalry between gifted English playboy James Hunt and his disciplined Austrian opponent, Niki Lauda. Taking us into their personal lives and clashes on and off the Grand Prix racetrack, Rush follows the two drivers as they push themselves to the breaking point of physical and psychological endurance, where there is no shortcut to victory and no margin for error.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$187,289 (0.7% of total gross)
Legs:2.69 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:27.5% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$38,000,000 (worldwide box office is 2.6 times production budget)
Theater counts:5 opening theaters/2,308 max. theaters, 4.0 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $39,642,508

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: September 20th, 2013 (Limited) by Universal
September 27th, 2013 (Expands Wide) by Universal
International Releases: September 13th, 2013 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
October 4th, 2013 (Wide) (Czech Republic)
December 11th, 2015 (Wide) (China)
July 10th, 2025 (Limited), released as Rush (Гонка) (Russia (CIS))
Video Release: January 28th, 2014 by Universal Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for sexual content, nudity, language, some disturbing images and brief drug use.
(Rating bulletin 2263, 3/13/2013)
Running Time: 123 minutes
Filming location (country): Austria, Germany, United Kingdom
Plot point: Auto Racing, Biography, Comeback, Medical and Hospitals, Professional Rivalry
Subgenre: Biographical Drama
Time period setting: 1970s
Source:Based on Real Life Events
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Dramatization
Production/Financing Companies: Universal Pictures, Cross Creek Pictures, Exclusive Media Group, Imagine Entertainment, Revolution Studios, Working Title Films
Production Countries: United States, United Kingdom
Languages: English, German, Italian

Synopsis

Set against the sexy and glamorous "golden age" of Formula 1 racing, Rush portrays the true story of the rivalry between gifted English playboy James Hunt and his disciplined Austrian opponent, Niki Lauda. Taking us into their personal lives and clashes on and off the Grand Prix racetrack, Rush follows the two drivers as they push themselves to the breaking point of physical and psychological endurance, where there is no shortcut to victory and no margin for error.
